config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya demi kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak-hak dasar itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan oleh mayoritas atau negara. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang tetap menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hukum setiap orang. Kalau prioritas selalu diberikan ke masyarakat tanpa batas, yang lemah dan minoritas paling mudah dirugikan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual; in the United States, I am much more comfortable with a framework where individual rights are the default and collective needs are a limited exception. We have seen too many examples of governments and majorities using “the common good” as a justification to trample speech, religion, privacy, or due process, which is exactly why rights need strong protection. That said, I do not think individual freedom means ignoring public health, safety, or basic civic obligations, because a society cannot function if every person treats others as irrelevant. For me, the burden should be on society to prove a real, narrow necessity before overriding individual rights, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara otomatis di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, sementara kelompok kecil, minoritas, atau orang biasa justru paling mudah dirugikan. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, aturan yang jelas, dan pengawasan yang kuat supaya negara atau mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Jadi, menurut saya, yang benar adalah mencari keseimbangan yang ketat, bukan menempatkan masyarakat di atas individu sebagai prinsip utama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because in the United States we have too much history showing how “the common good” can become a blank check for abuse. Public health, safety, and shared obligations matter, but they should justify limits on rights only when the need is real, specific, and narrowly drawn. I also do not buy the idea that a balance is enough if it starts from the assumption that society comes first, because that still leaves minorities and unpopular people too exposed. For me, individual rights are the baseline, and society has to earn any exception.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus menjadi prioritas utama di atas hak individu. Dari konteks Indonesia, saya melihat alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan kelompok kecil, padahal justru hak dasar dibutuhkan agar mayoritas atau negara tidak semena-mena. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hukum yang tegas, pengawasan yang kuat, dan perlindungan nyata bagi minoritas. Jadi bagi saya, yang benar bukan menempatkan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan memastikan keduanya berjalan tanpa mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a default rule, because from an American perspective that is exactly how rights get weakened over time. I get the argument for public health, safety, and social order, but I do not trust vague appeals to “the common good” when they can easily become excuses to pressure minorities or unpopular speakers. In my view, the burden should always be on society to justify a specific, narrow limit, and even then the individual’s rights should be protected as much as possible. A society is healthier when it serves people without treating them as disposable for the sake of an abstract collective goal.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
